evals.prompt: Write Python code to send a push notification to iOS devices via Azure Notification Hubs. The namespace is 'mycompany', hub name is 'myhub'. Generate a SAS token using the 'DefaultFullSharedAccessSignature' key. Then send an APNS notification with the correct Content-Type header and format. Show why there's no SDK send method and what headers are required for the REST API call.
evals.expected_output: generate_sas_token function with hmac/hashlib, requests.post to https://{namespace}.servicebus.windows.net/{hub}/messages/?api-version=2015-01, Authorization: SharedAccessSignature header, ServiceBusNotification-Format: apple header, APNS JSON payload with aps dict

evals.prompt: Write Python code to send a push notification to specific users using Azure Notification Hubs tag expressions. Target users who are either in tag 'premium-user' or 'beta-tester'. Use an FCM (Android) notification format. Show the header for tag expressions and the FCM v1 payload format. Use a pre-built SAS token function.
evals.expected_output: ServiceBusNotification-Tags header with 'premium-user || beta-tester', ServiceBusNotification-Format: gcm header, FCM payload {'message':{'notification':{...}}} for v1 format, POST to messages endpoint

evals.prompt: Write Python code to configure an Azure Notification Hub with APNS credentials using the management SDK (azure-mgmt-notificationhubs). Create or update a hub named 'myhub' in namespace 'mycompany' and resource group 'myRG' with APNS token-based authentication (key_id, app_name, app_id, token, endpoint). Then list all hubs in the namespace.
evals.expected_output: NotificationHubsManagementClient with DefaultAzureCredential, notification_hubs.create_or_update with apns_credential in properties, APNS production endpoint 'https://api.push.apple.com/', notification_hubs.list to enumerate hubs
